name: ArcLight
description: Classic GEVI using a VSD fused to pH-sensitive GFP.

spectrum.name: ArcLight
spectrum.note: ArcLight's fluorophore is super ecliptic pHluorin (A227D); the A227D mutation does not alter its ex/em spectrum (Jin 2012 Fig. S2). Super ecliptic pHluorin has no dedicated FPbase curve and is spectrally ≈ EGFP, so the verbatim FPbase EGFP spectrum is used as the proxy (ex corr 1.00, em corr 1.00). Same convention as the sibling Marina.

voltage.name: ArcLight
voltage.note: ArcLight (ArcLightD = A242) ΔF/F–V re-digitized from the normalized voltage-dependence curve in Milosevic 2020 Fig. 2C (−70 mV = 0%, +100 mV = 100%) and rescaled to the Fig. 2D amplitude (−29.6% ΔF/F for the standard −70→+30 mV 100-mV step): ΔF/F(V) = −0.41 × normalized(V). Replaces the previous curve, which was mis-sourced to Jin 2012 Fig. 1C (that figure shows the earlier ~18%/100mV super-ecliptic-pHluorin-A227D precursor, not A242; A242 has no F-V curve in the origin paper, only the Fig. 2C bar). −70 mV normalized to 0; the +100 mV (170-mV step) point is the least certain.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted means of the double-exponential fit for ArcLight A242 (the variant this page represents), Jin 2012 Suppl. Fig. 3B, HEK293 voltage clamp, bath 33–35°C (Methods): ON τ1 10 ms (amp 0.65)/τ2 53 ms → on = 0.65×10 + 0.35×53 = 25.1 ms; OFF τ1 22 ms (amp 0.70)/τ2 74 ms → off = 0.70×22 + 0.30×74 = 37.6 ms. CORRECTED: the previous entry stored the fast component of the WRONG variant (9/17 ms is ArcLight Q239, and was the fast component alone at a mislabeled '25°C'); the amplitudes ARE published in Fig. S3B and the A242 row is the correct one for this page. Main-text Fig. 1D (τfast ~10/τslow ~50) is the same ON response summarized.

28/104 ms are the bi-exponential FAST components (identical to the ncomms5625 measurement below: depol τfast 28 ms/τslow 271 ms, hyperpol τfast 104 ms/τslow 283 ms). Villette Table 1 reproduces these literature values; amplitude-weighted means are ~176/174 ms (see ncomms5625 entry). FLAG: likely a re-citation of the same measurement.

Amplitude-weighted means of the bi-exponential fit at 34°C (Hochbaum Suppl. Table 2, ArcLight A242, HEK293): τup τ1 12 ms (78%)/τ2 72 ms → on = 0.78×12 + 0.22×72 = 25.2 ms; τdown 21.5 ms (mono-exponential, 100% fast) → off = 21.5 ms. CORRECTED: the previous entry (15/45 ms) was mis-cited to St-Pierre nn.3709 Fig. 1e, which actually reports ArcLight Q239 activation τ1 44.6/τ2 273 ms (63.7% fast) at 22°C — neither matches 15/45. At 23°C Hochbaum Table S2 gives weighted ~82/108 ms.

Amplitude-weighted means of the bi-exponential fit: depolarizing τfast 28 ms (39%)/τslow 271 ms → on = 0.39×28 + 0.61×271 = 176 ms; hyperpolarizing τfast 104 ms (61%)/τslow 283 ms → off = 0.61×104 + 0.39×283 = 174 ms. Previously stored the fast components alone (28/104 ms).

dynamicRangeData.note: ArcLight A242 in HEK293T cells at 23°C, n=7 cells

brightnessData.note: Molar brightness at −70 mV from Villette et al. 2019 Table 1 = 22 mM⁻¹cm⁻¹ = 22000 M⁻¹cm⁻¹ (absolute); EGFP reference 56000 × 0.60 = 33600 → 22000/33600 ≈ 0.655. (Was 1.097, computed with the incorrect 20040 anchor.)

photobleach.note: Cross-GEVI value from the Bongwoori-R3 origin paper (Lee et al. 2017, primary subject Bongwoori-R3). Orange ▼ ArcLight A242 curve — the least photostable of the three at long times (~18% at 1540 s; t75 ≈ 177 s). This arclight.json page IS ArcLight A242. Re-digitized 2026-07-02 as part of a consistent tri-curve pass over Fig. S1 (all three curves re-traced together, axis-calibrated, overlay-verified against the figure); stretched-exponential fit (τ 751.7, b 0.861). The prior trace bled too low (tail 0.138, t75 146) — corrected to tail 0.177 / t75 177 so the companion overlay with Bongwoori (green, t75 124) and Bongwoori-R3 (purple, t75 295) is faithful. Origin-paper photobleach Fig. S1 (HEK293 cells, Xenon arc lamp, repeating 40 s on / 20 s off, measured every 40 s, 1 mW/mm² 1P). All three GEVIs were measured under IDENTICAL conditions, so this cross-overlay (shared source+Fig. S1) is genuinely iso-condition. The paper reports the photobleach τ is >450 s for all three but gives no exact per-GEVI value. intensityMWmm2 is omitted: 1 mW/mm² is 100× below the 100 mW/mm² reference, so linear dose-scaling would give a misleadingly tiny t75 — the value stands at native 1 mW/mm².

Cross-GEVI from the ASAP2s paper (primary subject ASAP2s); the sensor measured is 'ArcLight Q239', i.e. the original ArcLight this page represents. ArcLight (red) MONOexponential τ=224 s per Fig. 1-fig.suppl.3B (single-exp, unlike the biexponential ASAP sensors). t75≈64 s, t50≈155 s. Distinct from this page's other photobleach entry (Bongwoori-R3 paper Fig. S1, 1 mW/mm²). Origin-paper 1P photobleach, Fig. 1—figure supplement 3A: HEK293-Kir2.1 cells, continuous widefield 470–490 nm at 11 mW/mm², emission 525/50 nm, normalized to 1.0 at t=0. The stored curve is generated from the paper's OWN published fit (Fig. 1—figure supplement 3B) rather than pixel-digitized, because ASAP1/ASAP2s/ArcLight overlap almost exactly in panel A; the fit endpoints match the panel-A traces at 5 min. All co-plotted sensors (ASAP1, ASAP2s, ArcLight Q239; ASAP1::EGFP has no page) share this source+sourceFigure so they cross-overlay.

Cross-GEVI value from the Marina origin paper (Platisa 2017; primary subject Marina). Blue ArcLight curve digitized (HSV color separation) from the photobleaching panel Fig. 3C: Intensity (AU) vs cumulative excitation time (0–300 s, 60 cycles of 5 s on / 55 s off, 18 mW/mm² 488 nm widefield, HEK293 cells), normalized to F/F₀ = 1.0 at t = 0 (F₀ ≈ 6255 AU). ArcLight is the least photostable of the three curves in this figure, decaying to ~29% by 300 s; model-free t75 ≈ 42 s (biexponential fit; interpolated data crossing ~43 s), consistent with the paper's single-exponential τ = 132.3 s (→ t75 ≈ 38 s) in the early regime. intensityMWmm2 = 18 → dose-scaled t75 ≈ 7.6 s at the 100 mW/mm² reference. Co-plotted with Marina (a descendant variant of this sensor) from the same Fig. 3C (shared source + sourceFigure → cross-overlay). Distinct from this page's other two photobleach entries (Bongwoori-R3 Fig. S1 @ 1 mW/mm²; ASAP2s Fig. 1—fig. suppl. 3A @ 11 mW/mm²).

Cross-GEVI value from the FlicR1 origin paper (Abdelfattah 2016; primary subject FlicR1). The sensor is 'ArcLight Q239', i.e. the original ArcLight this page represents (black curve in Fig. 3I). Digitized (HSV/black color separation) from the 1P photobleaching panel Fig. 3I: normalized fluorescence vs time for FlicR1 (red), ArcLight Q239 (black), ASAP1 (green) in HEK293 cells under continuous 488 nm illumination at 10 W/cm² = 100 mW/mm², normalized to 1.0 at t=0. ArcLight is the MOST photostable of the three (slowest decay); model-free t75 ≈ 56 s (biexponential fit, a 0.441 / τ 130 / τ2 295 s), broadly consistent with the paper's single-exponential τ = 300 ± 30 s in the early regime (the real curve is somewhat steeper than that single-exp). Already at the 100 mW/mm² reference. RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-04 (flicr1 cross-check): the black ArcLight Q239 curve was re-digitized from Fig. 3I on a clean axis-calibrated grid — the prior trace over-read the mid-region (t=120 s 0.58→0.55; a spurious t=140 s 0.539 point that produced a flatten-then-drop shoulder was removed and replaced by t=150 s 0.476), giving a smoothly monotonic decay; t75 56 vs the prior 58 s. The companion FlicR1 red curve was corrected in the same panel. Distinct from this page's other photobleach entries (Bongwoori-R3 Fig. S1 @ 1 mW/mm²; ASAP2s Fig. 1—fig. suppl. 3A @ 11 mW/mm²; Marina Fig. 3C @ 18 mW/mm²). Cross-plotted with FlicR1 and ASAP1 from the same Fig. 3I.

apWidthData.sample: Cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: STATED optical single-AP FWHM for ArcLight Q239 (= the ArcLight this page represents) from St-Pierre 2014 Fig. 1f: 'the FWHM of the fluorescence responses were 3.7 ms and 6.5 ms for ASAP1 and ArcLight Q239, respectively' (current-injected APs in cultured hippocampal neurons; the co-measured electrical-trace FWHM was 3.6 ms). Cross-GEVI value (paper's primary subject is ASAP1). samplingRate omitted: the frame rate for this specific neuron panel is not separately reported (the paper images at 'kilohertz frame rates', 5–10 kHz for the HEK kinetics); the 6.5-ms width is partly kinetics-broadened (ArcLight has slow τ_off) but at the fast end it is not strictly comparable to frame-rate-specified widths without the rate. Patch-clamp performed at 22°C.

apWidthData.sample: Cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Cross-GEVI value from the FlicR1 origin paper (Abdelfattah 2016; primary subject FlicR1), 'ArcLight Q239' = the ArcLight this page represents. FWHM of the mean single-AP optical fluorescence waveform, derived by digitizing the ArcLight Q239 (green) trace in Fig. 5E (ΔF/F vs time over the paired electrical AP; 1 kHz frame rate, 10 W/cm² 488 nm, cultured rat hippocampal neurons). No numeric FWHM is stated; measured here as the width at half of the baseline-to-peak amplitude (peak ≈ 48 ms, half-max span ≈ 28.9–99.5 ms → FWHM ≈ 70.6 ms), overlay-verified. This is much broader than the co-measured FlicR1 (≈ 6.8 ms) — the paper explicitly notes the FlicR1 signal 'was not broadened to the extent that it was with ArcLight Q239'. The large FWHM (> the usual 1–20 ms range) is genuine, reflecting ArcLight's slow deactivation kinetics (τ_off tens–hundreds of ms). Recording temperature not stated for this neuron experiment.
